titre: Sharing economy in the era of full automation: Evidence from autonomous vehicle on-demand mobility services
resume_fr: [Extrait original, traduction non disponible] The digital age has facilitated the sharing of underutilized assets. This paper focuses on privately owned autonomous vehicles (AVs), a unique class of robots that can move independently and provide transportation services.
auteurs: Xiaoyan Wang, Kenan Zhang, Yaochen Ma

titre: Resample or Reroute? Budget-Aware Test-Time Model Selection for Large Language Models
resume_fr: [Extrait original, traduction non disponible] Routing among large language models (LLMs) trades response quality against serving cost, motivated by the reported gap between deployed routers and a per-instance oracle.

titre: WebSwarm: Recursive Multi-Agent Orchestration for Deep-and-Wide Web Search
resume_fr: [Extrait original, traduction non disponible] Large language model (LLM)-based web search agents are transforming information seeking from simple factoid question answering into complex, deep-and-wide search and research-oriented tasks.
auteurs: Xiaoshuai Song, Liancheng Zhang, Kangzhi Zhao, Yutao Zhu, Zhongyuan Wang, Guanting Dong, Jinghan Yang, Han Li, Kun Gai, Ji-Rong Wen, Zhicheng Dou

titre: Predicting Male Fertility Using Machine Learning: A Semen Parameters Based Analysis with the VISEM Dataset
resume_fr: [Extrait original, traduction non disponible] Male infertility is a significant yet often underdiagnosed aspect of reproductive health, with semen analysis serving as the cornerstone of clinical evaluation.
auteurs: Shahnawaz Qureshi, Raja Khurram Shahzad, Muhammad Fozan, Emal Kawal, Syed Aziz Shah, Sattam Al-Anazi, Syed MuhammadZeeshan Iqbal

titre: Token-Flow Firewall: Semantic Runtime Auditing for Persistent AI Agents
resume_fr: [Extrait original, traduction non disponible] Persistent AI agents extend large language models (LLMs) beyond single-turn interaction into long-lived software systems.
auteurs: Puji Wang, Yingchen Zhang, Ruqing Zhang, Jiafeng Guo, Xueqi Cheng

titre: Estimating Causal Effects from Data Generated by Stochastic Algorithms
resume_fr: [Extrait original, traduction non disponible] Recommendation systems and chatbots present content to users, typically using stochastic algorithms that select the content based on user characteristics or context. Examples of content include chat responses, videos, or items available for purchase.
auteurs: Susan Athey, Guido Imbens, Zoe Ji

titre: Forecasting Realized Volatility with Time Series Foundation Models: A Comparison with Econometric Benchmarks
resume_fr: [Extrait original, traduction non disponible] We ask whether pretrained time series foundation models (TSFMs) improve on established econometric benchmarks for forecasting realized volatility.

titre: ChatGPT’s financial advice: Supply, demand, and the life cycle
resume_fr: [Extrait original, traduction non disponible] The rise of generative AI has raised hopes that high-quality, personalised financial advice might finally become cheap and universally accessible. This column develops a method to quantify the impact of following AI financial advice over a lifetime.

titre: AI, productivity, and work: Evidence from US firms
resume_fr: [Extrait original, traduction non disponible] Real-time evidence from users can inform the current policy debates around artificial intelligence. This column reports on a survey of executives at typical US firms to reveal that AI adoption is already widespread but shallow.
